commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR lets non-default Telegram account forum topics with explicit topic `agentId` bypass the named-account fallback drop, adds root-message forum session-key behavior, and updates Telegram regression tests.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +84. Total +182 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: configure a non-default Telegram account forum topic with explicit topic `agentId`; current main resolves that route but then drops named-account group fallback before dispatch. The PR body also includes live before/after Telegram proof for that route.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can remove the unrelated root-message session-key behavior while preserving the intended named-account topic-agent guard.

target.title: fix(telegram): route account-scoped topic agents
target.body:
## Summary

- Allow non-default Telegram account topic routes with an explicit topic `agentId` to dispatch instead of being dropped as unnamed group fallback.
- Preserve the guard for non-default account group messages that do not have an explicit topic agent route.
- Add regression coverage for account-scoped topic agents and native Telegram reply-root session continuity.

## Reviewer replay

1. Configure a non-default Telegram account group topic route under `channels.telegram.accounts.<account-id>.groups.<group-id>.topics.<topic-id>` with `agentId` set and `requireMention=false`.
2. Send a message in that Telegram topic.
3. Verify the gateway logs an inbound message and dispatches a session instead of logging `telegram: drop non-default account requires explicit binding`.
4. Remove the topic `agentId` or test an unconfigured topic on the same non-default account group and verify the fallback drop still happens.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Account-scoped Telegram topic routes with an explicit topic `agentId` should dispatch to the configured agent on non-default Telegram accounts. Non-default account group fallback without an explicit topic route should still drop.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway rebuilt from this patch on macOS, connected to the existing Gartner Gary non-default Telegram account and the Gartner `/ Wiki` Telegram topic.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

1. Applied this patch locally.
2. Rebuilt OpenClaw and restarted the local gateway.
3. Retested the existing Gartner `/ Wiki` Telegram topic that had been failing on the non-default account route.
4. Sent a live smoke prompt to `@GartnerGaryBot` in that Telegram topic.

Evidence after fix:

Before the fix, the live gateway dropped messages in that topic before dispatch:

```text
telegram: drop non-default account requires explicit binding target=gartner-gary
```

After the fix, the same live Telegram topic dispatched to the topic agent route:

```text
Inbound message telegram:group:<redacted>:topic:2 -> @GartnerGaryBot
```

I then sent this live Telegram smoke prompt in the Gartner `/ Wiki` topic:

```text
@GartnerGaryBot post-fix smoke test: reply exactly GARTNER-WIKI-FIXED-OK. Make no file changes.
```

The bot replied in the same Telegram topic:

```text
GARTNER-WIKI-FIXED-OK
```

Observed result after fix: The same account-scoped Telegram topic that previously logged `drop non-default account requires explicit binding` dispatched to `@GartnerGaryBot`, and the bot replied in the live topic with `GARTNER-WIKI-FIXED-OK`.

What was not tested: I did not test every Telegram account or every topic route shape in a live environment. The regression suite covers the named-account fallback and explicit topic-agent branches.

## Verification

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.named-account-dm.test-support.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.require-mention.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.acp-bindings.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- Live Gartner `/ Wiki` topic smoke test replied `GARTNER-WIKI-FIXED-OK` after the runtime patch was applied locally.
target.author: codysai001

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
